title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition
content:
Charge-Offs & Recoveries
Year-to-date gross charge-offs totaled $65,000.
Losses remain concentrated in All Other Unsecured ($65), which accounted for the majority of total losses.
Recovery data was not disclosed in the source materials for this period,
and therefore no recovery figures are presented here.
Management continues to focus on collections and early-stage delinquency management as charge-off activity has declined compared with prior periods, reflecting improved portfolio performance despite a sharp reduction in unsecured lending volume.

Allowance & CECL Position
The CECL allowance decreased $201,000, representing a 12.4% decrease from the previous period. Reserve levels remain aligned with current portfolio risk characteristics and declining charge-off activity, while also accounting for the contraction in overall loan balances. Reserve assumptions continue to incorporate both historical loss experience and forward-looking economic expectations.
Reserve levels address:
- Risk concentrations within All Other Unsecured portfolios
- Current delinquency and charge-off trends
- Expected future credit conditions and portfolio performance
Management continues to evaluate CECL assumptions and loss factors to ensure reserve adequacy remains appropriate for the evolving risk profile of the loan portfolio.

Overall Assessment
Loan performance reflects manageable credit risk despite continued contraction in loan balances. Delinquencies remain concentrated within All Other Unsecured, while charge-off activity has declined compared with prior periods. The portfolio continues to be supported by adequate capital and limited reserve coverage, matched to the actual reserve trend. Management remains focused on disciplined underwriting, proactive collection efforts, and ongoing portfolio monitoring to preserve asset quality while supporting future loan growth and earnings stability.
